helpers.name: aimfp_run

parameters.name: is_new_session
parameters.description: Whether this is a new session start. Use true for first interaction, new session, or after long breaks. Use false (default) for continuation work.

helpers.purpose: Main entry point orchestrator. Called on session start (is_new_session=true) for full context bundle, and at work checkpoints (is_new_session=false) for watchdog alerts only.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error message if database not accessible. Returns empty suggestions if no directives match.
return_statements: If user setting project_continue_on_start=true and is_new_session=true: Automatically continue project work with bundled context instead of waiting for user command
When autostart enabled: Load pending tasks from status, present with priority order, proactively suggest next steps for project completion

DB-FIRST LOOKUP POLICY: Throughout this session, use get_function_by_name, get_type_by_name, get_file_by_name, get_functions_by_file to research existing code BEFORE reading source files. The project DB is a queryable index of all tracked files, functions, and types — use it as your first stop for lookups. Only read source files when the DB doesn't answer your question or you need implementation details beyond signatures.

FLOWS & THEMES: Before starting work on any task, check get_all_flows() and get_all_themes() for relevant workflow descriptions. Flows describe the intended architecture and user-facing behavior your implementation should match.
If data.migration.pending is non-empty, inform user and call migrate_databases(project_root) to upgrade project databases to current schema versions
If data.deferred_notes is non-empty, review deferred work items and consider whether any should be addressed in the current session

NOTES REVIEW: Before starting any task or user request, search_notes(search_string=<relevant keywords>, exclude_note_types=['completed','obsolete']) for prior decisions, deferred work, or warnings. Empty = proceed. Hits = review before coding. When a reviewed note is already handled, update_note(id, note_type='completed' or 'obsolete') to keep notes clean.

TRACKING REMINDER: After ANY code change (even small fixes, param tweaks, refactors), review whether the change is database-worthy: file modified? function added/changed? parameters or return types altered? If YES — track immediately. Do not skip, do not ask, do not defer. 'Small' does not mean 'untracked.'

MODULES: Review modules_summary[] for code boundary awareness. Each module has name, path, purpose, and file_count. When modifying files, respect module boundaries — use get_module_for_file() to check membership and get_module_dependencies() to understand cross-module impacts.

WATCHDOG REMINDERS: If watchdog.reminders is non-empty, review and handle each finding (deleted files, unregistered files, missing functions). Once all reminders have been addressed, call clear_watchdog() to acknowledge and clear them. Reminders persist until cleared — do not ignore them.
implementation_notes: Returns: If is_new_session=true → bundles {status, user_settings, fp_directive_index, all_directive_names[], modules_summary[], infrastructure_data, guidance, watchdog: {started, start_error, status, reminders[], notice}}
Returns: If is_new_session=false → watchdog alerts only {watchdog: {status, reminders[], notice}, notice} when reminders exist, empty {} when nothing to report.

Watchdog status values: 'ok' (running, checked), 'not_running' (PID file missing — inform user), 'no_reminders_file' (PID exists but reminders missing — watchdog may have failed), 'no_project' (no project found). AI should surface notice to user when status is not 'ok'.

Session bundle calls: aimfp_status(), get_user_settings(), get_fp_directive_index(), get_all_directive_names(), get_all_infrastructure(), check_and_run_backup(). Note: supportive_context and modules_summary are included via aimfp_status() — promoted to top-level for startup visibility.
Directive names only - NOT full directive data (AI queries by name when needs details)
fp_directive_index: FP directive names grouped by category for quick reference
all_directive_names: Complete list of ALL directive names (project, fp, user, git) for AI to know what's available
Does NOT parse user input or match intent - returns data only
AI receives guidance on session start and makes own decisions about which directives to apply
Continuation calls (is_new_session=false) return empty when watchdog has nothing — near-zero token cost
Session bundle is ~16-20k tokens: status (~10k, includes supportive_context) + settings (~1k) + fp_index (~2k) + directive_names (~1k) + infrastructure (~500) + guidance (~2k)
Always includes guidance on when to call aimfp_run(is_new_session=true) again

used_by_directives.description: aimfp_run directive uses aimfp_run helper as entry point orchestrator

helpers.name: aimfp_init
helpers.purpose: Phase 1 mechanical setup orchestrator for project initialization. Atomically creates directories, databases with schemas, and templates. No deep logic - pure mechanical operations.
helpers.error_handling: Return detailed error if: already initialized, directory permissions denied, database creation failed. Clean up partial initialization on error (delete .aimfp-project/ if incomplete).
return_statements: Verify initialization: confirm .aimfp-project/ directory exists with project.db, user_preferences.db, ProjectBlueprint.md, and backups/ directory. Check project.db has 21 tables, user_preferences.db has 8 tables, infrastructure has 8 entries. Report any issues to user before proceeding.

Phase 1 complete. AI must now execute Phase 2: scan for OOP patterns (if existing code), detect infrastructure (language, build tool, source directory), and prompt user for project name/purpose/goals. IMPORTANT: The project table is EMPTY after Phase 1. AI must call create_project(name, purpose, goals, status, version) as the FIRST step of Phase 2 to insert the project row. Do NOT call update_project before create_project — there is no row to update yet.

Phase 2 ordering: (1) create_project — must complete first, (2) update_source_directory + update infrastructure entries — can run in parallel after create_project succeeds, (3) populate ProjectBlueprint.md. Do NOT batch create_project with infrastructure updates in one parallel call.

Empty infrastructure entries created: project_root (populated), source_directory, primary_language, build_tool, package_manager, test_framework, runtime_version, main_branch. AI must detect and populate these using update_project_entry(table='infrastructure', id=N, data={'value': '...'}) — only the 'value' field should be updated (not 'description' or 'details'). Call get_all_infrastructure() first to see current IDs and values.
ProjectBlueprint.md is a template with placeholders. AI must populate with real project data after user interaction.

Git Repository Status: check data.git_status — 'created' means .git was initialized during Phase 1, 'pre_existing' means repository already existed, 'git_unavailable' means git command not found or failed. If 'created' or 'pre_existing', the git_init directive must be executed during project_discovery to complete intelligent Git setup (gitignore, initial commit, hash storage, branch detection). If 'git_unavailable', inform user that version control is unavailable and suggest installing git.

After Phase 2 population complete: route to project_discovery directive. Discovery handles: git_init directive (intelligent Git setup if git available), collaborative blueprint refinement, infrastructure confirmation, state database creation (via create_state_database helper once source_directory is confirmed), theme/flow definition, completion path with milestones.

Do NOT create state database during init — source_directory is not yet confirmed. State DB creation happens during project_discovery Branch 3 (map_infrastructure) after source_directory is confirmed with user.
.watchdogignore created at project root.
implementation_notes: PHASE 1 ONLY - Mechanical setup with no intelligence
Step 1: Check if already initialized (error if .aimfp-project/project.db exists)
Step 2: Create directories: .aimfp-project/, .aimfp-project/backups/

Step 2.5: Check for .git directory. If missing and git available, run 'git init' (subprocess, non-blocking, 10s timeout). Store result as git_status: 'created' (new repo), 'pre_existing' (already had .git), or 'git_unavailable' (git not found or failed). Initialization continues regardless of git outcome.
Step 3: Copy ProjectBlueprint_template.md to .aimfp-project/ProjectBlueprint.md

Step 4: Initialize project.db: load schemas/project.sql, execute, then execute initialization/standard_infrastructure.sql (8 empty entries), then call update_project_root() to populate project_root value
Step 5: Initialize user_preferences.db: load schemas/user_preferences.sql, execute, INSERT default tracking_settings (all disabled), load initialization/standard_user_settings.sql (backup defaults)
Step 6: Create .gitkeep files for empty directories
Step 6.5: Write .watchdogignore to project root from DEFAULT_WATCHDOGIGNORE_CONTENT (watchdog/config.py). Idempotent — skip if it already exists.
Step 7: Verify all created files exist (inline check, no helper call)
Step 8: Return success object

RETURN STRUCTURE: {success: bool, project_root: str, aimfp_dir: str, files_created: ['.aimfp-project/', 'project.db', 'user_preferences.db', 'ProjectBlueprint.md', '.watchdogignore'], tables_created: {project_db: [...], user_prefs_db: [...]}, infrastructure_entries: 8, git_status: 'created'|'pre_existing'|'git_unavailable', next_phase: 'AI populates infrastructure and blueprint'}
State database is NOT created during init. It is created during project_discovery (Branch 3: map_infrastructure) via create_state_database() helper after source_directory is confirmed with user.
State DB reference: SQL schema at src/aimfp/database/initialization/state_db.sql, template at src/aimfp/templates/state_db/state_operations.py, helper at helpers/project/state_db.py
ERROR CLEANUP: If any step fails after Step 2, delete .aimfp-project/ directory entirely (shutil.rmtree). Partial initialization is worse than no initialization.
ERROR RETURN: {success: false, error: str, failed_step: int, cleanup_performed: bool}
All operations are direct file/DB operations - no external helper calls

parameters_mapping.project_root: from current working directory or user input

used_by_directives.description: aimfp_init directive calls aimfp_init for Phase 1 mechanical setup

helpers.name: aimfp_status
helpers.purpose: Status orchestrator that retrieves comprehensive project state. Gathers data from multiple tables for AI to determine next steps.
helpers.error_handling: Returns {initialized: false} if .aimfp-project/ does not exist.
return_statements: AI should interpret status data and determine next directive to execute based on project state.
If not initialized: route to aimfp_init directive.
If validation has errors: AI should address structural issues before continuing work.
Priority order for current focus: sidequests → subtasks → tasks.

FLOWS & THEMES: Review get_all_flows() and get_all_themes() to orient on project architecture before diving into work. Flows describe intended user-facing behavior and implementation architecture. Themes identify project areas. Consult these when picking up work or starting a new task.
implementation_notes: Returns status object with: project_metadata, infrastructure, work_hierarchy (counts + tree, including modules count), user_directives_status, recent_warnings, git_state, modules_summary[], supportive_context
Calls get_project_status(project_root, type) for work hierarchy data (includes both flat counts AND nested tree)

Calls get_supportive_context() to include detailed FP examples, DRY patterns, state DB usage, behavioral rules — provides same reference material as aimfp_run(is_new_session=true) so AI can refresh context mid-session without full directive reload
AI assesses structural health from returned data (infrastructure, metadata, work_hierarchy)
Reads project metadata from project table
Reads infrastructure from infrastructure table
Checks project.user_directives_status for Use Case determination
If Use Case 2 + active: queries user_directives.db for active directive counts
Reads recent notes (limit 5, severity warning/error)
Gets git state from work_branches table
Type='quick': Counts and current work item only
Type='summary': Standard detail - incomplete items, recent context (default)
Type='detailed': Full data including all history
NO suggestion logic - AI determines appropriate next directive from returned data

used_by_directives.description: aimfp_status directive uses aimfp_status helper to retrieve comprehensive project state - AI determines next steps from returned data
Called by aimfp_run when is_new_session=true - provides comprehensive status for fresh context

helpers.name: get_project_status
helpers.purpose: Retrieve work hierarchy data from project database. Returns structured counts, records, and nested tree in a single pass.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error if project.db not found or not accessible.
return_statements: Files and functions may be reserved (is_reserved=1) but not finalized. Complete the reserve → write with ID in name → finalize flow before continuing.

Check reserved_entities for any functions/types with null fields (purpose, parameters, returns for functions; description for types). Populate missing fields via update_function/update_type before continuing.
implementation_notes: Summary returns: {counts{completion_paths, milestones, tasks, subtasks, sidequests, incomplete_*, blocked_items, modules}, hierarchy{active_path, active_milestone, active_tasks[], active_subtasks[], active_sidequests[], current_focus, active_items[], historical{}, recent_files[], reserved_entities{}}, blocked_items[]}
Hierarchy follows: completion_path → milestone → task/subtask/sidequest → items
Historical context is positional: last completed task in active milestone + items, last completed milestone if first task or no tasks
Reserved entities: files/functions with is_reserved=1 (not yet finalized)
Recent files: last 5 by updated_at
Detailed returns: full history with all records + nested tree
Type='quick': Counts and current in_progress item only
Type='summary': Hierarchy-aware context with positional history (default)
Type='detailed': All records including completed history + complete tree
NO analysis logic - returns raw data, AI interprets meaning

used_by_directives.description: Sub-helper used by aimfp_status to retrieve work hierarchy data (completion paths, milestones, tasks, subtasks, sidequests) with counts and nested tree

helpers.name: get_task_context

parameters.name: task_type
parameters.description: Type of task: 'task', 'subtask', 'sidequest'. Auto-detected from task_id if omitted.

helpers.purpose: Get complete context for resuming work on a specific task/subtask/sidequest. Single call retrieves item + associated items + flows + files + functions + optionally interactions and notes. Uses cached project root internally.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error if task not found or project.db not accessible.
return_statements: Task context loaded. AI should review items to determine where to resume.
If task is blocked: check blocked_by field and resolve blockers first.

BEFORE RESUMING WORK: Review the flows[] in the returned context — they describe the intended architecture and user-facing behavior. Use the returned functions[] data (purpose, parameters, returns) as your lookup index BEFORE reading source files.
MODULES: Review modules[] to see which code boundaries the task touches. If multiple modules appear, consider cross-module impacts via get_module_dependencies().
implementation_notes: Returns: {task_item{}, task_type, items[], flows[], files[], functions[], modules[] (distinct modules owning task files), interactions[]?, notes[]?}
If task_type omitted: searches tasks, subtasks, sidequests tables by task_id and auto-detects type
Uses get_cached_project_root() — no project_root parameter needed
Gets associated items from items table (polymorphic: reference_table + reference_id)
Gets flows linked to task's items via file_flows
Gets files from file_flows for those flows
Gets functions for those files
If include_interactions=true: gets interactions for those functions
If include_history=true: gets notes for this task
Optimized for session resumption - all context in one call

used_by_directives.description: Load complete task context for resuming interrupted work - retrieves task details, associated flows, files, functions, interactions, and note history in single optimized query

helpers.name: get_current_progress
helpers.purpose: Single entry point for scoped project status queries. Replaces 5-10 separate helper calls with one flexible query.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error if project.db not found. Returns empty results for unknown scope.
implementation_notes: Returns: Flexible object based on scope and detail_level parameters
Routes to appropriate table queries based on scope value
Applies filters as WHERE conditions
Default detail_level='standard' returns reasonable data without overwhelming
scope=null returns summary counts for all entity types

helpers.name: update_project_state

parameters.name: create_note
parameters.description: Auto-log action as note in notes table

helpers.purpose: Orchestrated state change for tasks, milestones, and paths. Maps actions (start_task, complete_task, block_task, etc.) to correct status updates and note creation.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error if entity not found or invalid action for entity type.
return_statements: State updated. If task completed: check if all tasks in milestone are complete, consider marking milestone complete.
If task blocked: create sidequest to resolve blocker, then unblock and resume.

If task started (action=start_task): BEFORE CODING — check get_all_flows() and get_task_flows(task_id) for relevant workflow descriptions. Use DB lookups (get_function_by_name, get_type_by_name, get_functions_by_file) to understand existing code BEFORE reading source files.
implementation_notes: Returns: {success, updated_entity{}, note_created?, note_id?}
Maps action to appropriate field updates (status, timestamps)

Actions: start_task, complete_task, pause_task, resume_task, block_task, unblock_task, start_subtask, complete_subtask, start_sidequest, complete_sidequest, start_milestone, complete_milestone, start_path, complete_path
Updates entity status field and related timestamps
If create_note=true: inserts note record automatically with action context
Simplifies common state transitions with action-based interface

helpers.name: batch_update_progress

parameters.name: updates
parameters.description: List of update objects: [{target_type, target_id, action, data}, ...]

parameters.name: continue_on_error
parameters.description: Stop on first error or continue processing remaining updates

helpers.purpose: Update multiple project items atomically. Used after code generation or bulk operations to ensure consistency.
helpers.error_handling: Returns per-update results. If transaction=true and any fail, rolls back all.
return_statements: Batch update complete. Review any failures and retry individually if needed.
implementation_notes: Returns: {success, updated_count, failed_count, results[{success, entity, error?}]}
Iterates updates, calls update_project_state for each
Transaction=true: wraps in DB transaction for atomicity
Continue_on_error=true: processes all updates even if some fail
If transaction=true and any fail: rolls back all changes

helpers.name: query_project_state

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description: WHERE-like conditions with operators: {field: value} or {field: {op: 'gt', value: 5}}

parameters.name: joins
parameters.description: Relations to include via LEFT JOIN: ['milestones', 'files']

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: ORDER BY clause: 'field ASC' or 'field DESC'

helpers.purpose: Flexible SQL-like query interface for complex project queries. Provides powerful filtering and joining capabilities without writing raw SQL.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error for unknown entity names or invalid filter operators.
implementation_notes: Returns: Array of entity objects with joins embedded
Filter operators: eq, ne, gt, lt, gte, lte, in, like
Joins uses predefined LEFT JOIN mappings (e.g., entity='tasks' + joins=['milestones'] -> LEFT JOIN milestones ON tasks.milestone_id = milestones.id)
Sort: 'field ASC' or 'field DESC'
Powerful SQL-like interface without writing raw SQL
Prevents SQL injection by parameterizing all values

helpers.name: get_files_by_flow_context

parameters.name: flow_id
parameters.description: Flow ID to get files for

helpers.purpose: Get all files for a flow with functions embedded in each file dict.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error if flow not found or project.db not accessible.
implementation_notes: Returns: Array of file objects with functions[] embedded in each
Step 1: Query file_flows for flow_id -> get file_ids
Step 2: Query files for those file_ids
Step 3: For each file: query functions WHERE file_id = file.id
Step 4: Embed functions array in each file dict
Provides complete view of flow implementation

used_by_directives.description: Get all files implementing a flow with embedded functions - used to build dependency map showing file-flow-function relationships

helpers.name: create_project_backup
helpers.purpose: Automated backup sub-helper. Checks project inactivity against backup_duration setting, creates zip backup of .aimfp-project/ (excluding backups/ and watchdog/), generates backup_summary.md, and rotates old backups per backup_count setting.
helpers.error_handling: Returns {checked: true, triggered: false, reason: str} on any failure. Never throws — all errors are caught and returned as reason strings.
implementation_notes: Sub-helper: not exposed as MCP tool, called only by aimfp_run orchestrator during is_new_session=true
Reads backup settings from user_preferences.db user_settings table: backup_count (default 3), backup_duration (default 30 days), backup_path (default .aimfp-project)
Queries last activity timestamp across all project.db tables (notes, files, functions, tasks, milestones, subtasks, sidequests, project)
If last_activity older than backup_duration days: creates zip backup
Zip contents: all .aimfp-project/ files EXCEPT backups/ and watchdog/ directories + auto-generated backup_summary.md
Backup naming: aimfp-backup-YYYY-MM-DD.zip (or aimfp-backup-YYYY-MM-DD-HH-MM.zip if same-day duplicate)
Rotation: keeps only backup_count most recent zip files, deletes oldest
Returns: {checked: true, triggered: bool, backup_result: {created, backup_path, backup_name, rotated_count, error} or null, reason: str}

used_by_directives.description: Sub-helper called by aimfp_run during is_new_session=true to automatically backup .aimfp-project/ when project has been inactive beyond the configured threshold

helpers.name: migrate_databases
helpers.purpose: Migrate all pending project databases to current schema versions. Creates fresh DBs from new schema SQL, transfers data via ATTACH, returns temp paths for AI to verify and place.
helpers.error_handling: Returns per-DB results with verification data. Never throws — errors are captured per-DB.
return_statements: Verify all tables in each migrated DB show match=true in verification data
Move each backup from temp path to {project_root}/{aimfp_folder}/backups/{db_name}_pre_migration_v{old_version}_{YYYYMMDD}.db
Replace each old DB with new: move new_db_temp_path to original_db_path
Ask user about backup retention (delete old backups, keep, or cycle)
implementation_notes: Reads expected_schema_versions from aimfp_core.db to determine which DBs need migration
For each pending DB: backup old → create new from schema → ATTACH old → transfer shared columns → verify row counts
Schema-only: no init SQL (standard_infrastructure.sql etc.) — data comes from old DB
Triggers are AFTER UPDATE only, so INSERT transfers preserve original timestamps
Uses INSERT OR IGNORE to handle any constraint changes gracefully
Returns temp paths — tool does NOT move files, AI handles placement via return_statements

RETURN STRUCTURE: {migrated: [{db_name, old_version, new_version, backup_temp_path, new_db_temp_path, original_db_path, verification: {table: {old_rows, new_rows, match}}, warnings}], skipped: [], already_current: []}

helpers.name: aimfp_end
helpers.purpose: Session termination orchestrator. Gathers session audit data: reads watchdog state and final reminders, queries all files and functions from DB for AI to cross-reference, reads recent notes and task state. Stops watchdog process if running. Returns structured audit data for AI to interpret and act on. No analysis logic — AI determines what is stale or missing.
helpers.error_handling: Returns {initialized: false} if .aimfp-project/ does not exist. Returns partial data if watchdog PID file missing (watchdog not implemented or not running). Never fails hard — always returns what it can gather.
return_statements: AI should compare returned file/function lists against session work to identify untracked items.
AI should review returned task/milestone state for any needed progress updates.
AI should use returned watchdog reminders (if any) to address outstanding issues.
AI should generate session summary from returned data and present to user.
AI handles all FP compliance checking — helper only returns code data for AI to review.
Once complete, Inform user safe to end session.
implementation_notes: NO analysis or compliance logic — pure data gathering and process management

Step 1: Read .aimfp-project/watchdog/watchdog.pid — if PID file exists and process alive, read .aimfp-project/watchdog/reminders.json, then kill process. If PID file absent, set watchdog_stopped=null (not implemented/not running)
Step 2: Query files table — SELECT id, path, updated_at, is_reserved FROM files
Step 3: Query functions table — SELECT id, name, file_id, is_reserved FROM functions
Step 4: Query active tasks/items — SELECT from tasks, items WHERE status='in_progress'
Step 5: Query recent notes — SELECT from notes ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10
Step 6: Read project metadata — SELECT status, version from project table
Step 7: Return structured audit data object
RETURN STRUCTURE: {success: bool, watchdog: {stopped: bool|null, final_reminders: []}, project_state: {files: [], functions: [], active_tasks: [], active_items: [], recent_notes: [], metadata: {}}}
ERROR RETURN: {success: false, error: str, partial_data: {}|null}
Watchdog handling is graceful: if watchdog module not yet implemented, PID file won't exist — return watchdog.stopped=null
All operations are DB reads + one process kill — no writes except clearing reminders.json

parameters_mapping.project_root: from project context or user input

used_by_directives.description: aimfp_end directive calls aimfp_end helper to gather session audit data — AI interprets returned data and performs compliance checks, identifies gaps, generates summary

helpers.name: clear_watchdog
helpers.purpose: Clear watchdog reminders after they have been reviewed and handled. Reminders persist in .aimfp-project/watchdog/reminders.json until this is called. Call after addressing all watchdog findings (file deletions, unregistered files, etc.).
helpers.error_handling: Returns error if project root not established
return_statements: Watchdog reminders cleared. Resume normal workflow.

metadata.status: updated 2026-01-31 - moved get_supportive_context to helpers-index.json (not an orchestrator)
changes: Removed get_status_tree: tree logic folded into get_project_status
Removed get_project_context: redundant with existing helpers (get_from_project, get_all_infrastructure, get_project_status)
Renamed target_database 'orchestrator' to 'multi_db' for cross-DB helpers (aimfp_run, aimfp_init, aimfp_status)
Renamed target_database 'orchestrator' to 'project' for single-DB helpers (all others)
Added project_root parameter to helpers that were missing it
Updated aimfp_status implementation_notes to remove get_status_tree reference
Updated aimfp_init return_statements per orchestrator implementation plan
Updated file_path values to match planned file organization
Removed validate_initialization: validation logic belongs to AI, not coded helpers. aimfp_init uses inline file-exists check instead
Renamed get_work_context → get_task_context, work_item_type → task_type, work_item_id → task_id
Restructured get_project_status summary mode: hierarchy-aware context (completion_path → milestone → task → items) with positional history, reserved entities, recent files
Fixed items JOIN_MAPPINGS: items uses polymorphic reference_table + reference_id, not FK columns
metadata.notes: All helpers are data retrieval/update operations. NO analysis/suggestion logic - AI interprets returned data and decides next steps.
